zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles於1276年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利時，她的父親Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond31歲，母親Lady Juliane FitzMaurice14歲。她於1295年11月12日與Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford在英國英格蘭賀福爾克利福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1327年5月24日在英國英格蘭賀福爾海伊享年51歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles于1276年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利时，她的父亲Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond31岁，母亲Lady Juliane FitzMaurice14岁。她于1295年11月12日与Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford在英国英格兰賀福爾克利福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1327年5月24日在英国英格兰賀福爾海伊享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Baroness
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Norwegian: old variant of Bartnes (and, in North America, probably also an altered form of this), a habitational name from Trøndelag, a form of Old Norse burkn ‘fern’ + nes ‘promontary, headland’.
In some cases possibly also an altered form of German   Bartmess  .

variants.name: Bartmess
meanings: American shortened form of German   Barthelmess  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Welles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Wells  .

variants.name: Wells
meanings: English: habitational name from Wells next the Sea (Norfolk) or Wells (Somerset), both named with the plural of Old English wella ‘spring, stream’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a group of springs or streams.
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dupuis   ‘from the well’.
History: One of numerous early immigrants from England bearing this name was Thomas Welles, governor of colonial CT, who was in Hartford, CT, by 1636.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles was born in 1276, in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, her father, Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond, was 31 and her mother, Lady Juliane FitzMaurice, was 14. She married Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford on 12 November 1295, in Clifford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 24 May 1327, in Hay, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles est née en 1276, à Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Juliane FitzMaurice, avait 14 ans. Elle a épousé Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford le 12 novembre 1295, à Clifford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 24 mai 1327, à Hay, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles 1276 in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond 31 und ihre Mutter Lady Juliane FitzMaurice 14. Sie hat am 12. November 1295 in Clifford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 24. Mai 1327 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Hay, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles è nata il nel 1276 nel Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Lady Juliane FitzMaurice, aveva 14 anni. Lei sposato Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford il 12 novembre 1295, nel Clifford, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 24 maggio 1327, nel Hay, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud de Clare Baroness de Wellesが1276年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomondは31歳，母Lady Juliane FitzMauriceは14歳であった。1295年11月12日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクリフォードでRobert de Clifford 1st Baron de Cliffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1327年5月24日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアヘイで51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles(이)가 1276년 영국 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Juliane FitzMaurice(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 1295년 11월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 클리퍼드에서 Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1327년 5월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 헤이에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles nasceu em 1276, em Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Juliane FitzMaurice, tinha 14 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford em 12 de novembro de 1295, em Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 24 de maio de 1327, em Hay, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles родилась в 1276 г. в местности тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Juliane FitzMaurice, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford 12 ноября 1295 г. в местности клиффорд, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 24 мая 1327 г. в местности хеи, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 51 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles nació en 1276, en Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lady Juliane FitzMaurice, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford el 12 de noviembre de 1295, en Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 24 de mayo de 1327, en Hay, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 51 años.
